title: Poor Quality NGE and EoE DVDs
display_title: Poor Quality NGE and EoE DVDs
wikitext:
[]: Poor Quality NGE and EoE DVDs             Quote Post by OndrejTrhon » Thu Mar 31, 2011 9:58 am Hi, recetly, I have ordered a pack of Neon Genesis Evangelion Series together with End of Evangelion + Death and Rebirth pack for a great price. I was really looking forward to seeing NGE and EoE on my TV and i wanted to support its creators (even little much) but i was REALLY DISSAPOINTED. You can check how the packaging looks like in this gallery (czech domain, so maybe take some time to load and the pictures are big): [url]http://trhon.cz/wargaming/galerie/?level=album&id=3[/url] Before I decided to buy this, i watched NGE and EoE from my computer, because I downloaded it in great-quality MKV (I wanted to try it before spending money) and the DVDs are real crap compared to MKV. Not even the sound si poor compared to MKVs, put the picture (description was not mentioning that) is MPEG-2 Dvd Video hybrid or something and it looks horribly (look into spoiler below). Subtitles are ugly and as I've seen, it's something like weird French TV version (with French titles during intro and outro). Neon Genesis Evangelion MKV / DVD SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Thu Mar 31, 2011 10:00 am You bought a bootleg version, which is why you got such a great deal. I don't know if you'll get your money back. You'd be better off getting the legit version from Amazon or something. As for the letterboxing of EoE, the official U.S. release looked like that. You'll have to get the version from Japan to get a proper anamorphic version.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Mar 31, 2011 11:05 am That's most definitely a bootleg. If you're looking for an official English language release, I recommend the Platinum Thinpak: http://www.evamonkey.com/reviews_platinum_thinpak.php It is sometimes referred to as "Platinum Complete". As for the movies, sadly, the official DVDs are lousy. But they're so out of print, I actually recommend tracking down the Zhentarim Fansub . Alternatively, you can buy a Japanese release. Otherwise, you have to eBay a used copy.

[]:             Quote Post by OndrejTrhon » Thu Mar 31, 2011 12:38 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: That's most definitely a bootleg. If you're looking for an official English language release, I recommend the Platinum Thinpak: http://www.evamonkey.com/reviews_platinum_thinpak.php It is sometimes referred to as "Platinum Complete". As for the movies, sadly, the official DVDs are lousy. But they're so out of print, I actually recommend tracking down the Zhentarim Fansub . Alternatively, you can buy a Japanese release. Otherwise, you have to eBay a used copy.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Mar 31, 2011 12:45 pm OndrejTrhon wrote: The Platinum is really hard to buy, because i need a region free or region 2 copy. But do you have any reference to the Ultimate Tin Box? It can be bought here in Czech Republic for about one hundred USD (converted)...

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Thu Mar 31, 2011 1:41 pm OndrejTrhon wrote: i need a region free or region 2 copy

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Mar 31, 2011 4:40 pm Platinum got an R2 release from ADV (as had the earlier OA and follow-up DC episodes). There are the usual inevitable NTSC to PAL issues, but that's it. http://www.amazon.co.uk/s/ref=nb_sb_ss_i_8_19?url=search-alias%3Ddvd&field-keywords=evangelion+platinum&sprefix=evangelion+platinum

[]:             Quote Post by MasterRah » Thu Mar 31, 2011 6:00 pm Sorry man, but you got played big time. I remember people talking about Manga's release of End Of Eva, but I didn't realize that the original 16:9 video was letterboxed. What sense does that make? When I first watched it on Veoh, I just thought it was horrible DVDcopying. I was gonna recommend you this boxset... http://www.rightstuf.com/cgi-bin/catalogmgr/fbZx2OINhGZzelXiE8/browse/item/81583/4/0/0 I think it has all the extra features, and more not present in the Platinum Thinpack.

[]:             Quote Post by Fallingtogrey » Thu Mar 31, 2011 9:12 pm Possibly.

[]:             Quote Post by OndrejTrhon » Fri Apr 01, 2011 12:22 am Thanks for answers and helping! I am sure I will find a way how to buy the right release. Today, I am gonna send the dvds back, seller accept returns within 7 days, so i am safe (apart from paying postage of course). Again, thanks to all of you.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Apr 01, 2011 10:53 am OndrejTrhon wrote: Again, thanks to all of you.
